intended_use: Trained medical professionals use Contour ProtégéAI as a tool to assist in the automated processing of digital medical images of modalities CT and MR, as supported by ACR/NEMA DICOM 3.0. Creation of c
intended_use_full: Trained medical professionals use Contour ProtégéAI as a tool to assist in the automated processing of digital medical images of modalities CT and MR, as supported by ACR/NEMA DICOM 3.0. Creation of contours using machine-learning algorithms for applications including, but not limited to, quantitative analysis, aiding adaptive therapy, transferring contours to radiation therapy treatment planning systems, and archiving contours for patient follow-up and management. Segmenting anatomical structures across a variety of CT anatomical locations. And segmenting the prostate, the seminal vesicles, and the urethra within T2-weighted MR images.
target_condition: Patients requiring anatomical structure segmentation for radiation therapy planning, quantitative analysis, and treatment follow-up across multiple anatomical locations (head and neck, thorax, abdomen, pelvis, whole body)
ground_truth: Manual segmentations by consultants (physicians and dosimetrists) following published clinical contouring guidelines, reviewed and corrected by a radiation oncologist, with final review by qualified MIM Software staff (M.D. or licensed dosimetrists)
other_metrics: Dice coefficient (mean range 0.02–0.98 depending on structure); Mean Distance to Agreement (MDA) in mm (range 0.31–22.23 mm); Added Path Length (APL) in mm cumulative (Head and Neck: 28.61 ± 29.59 mm; Thorax: 65.44 ± 68.85 mm; Whole Body: 98.20 ± 127.11 mm); Localization accuracy (64–100% of images); User evaluation score on 3-point scale (range 2.0–3.0)
